description_long: This object is a long, antique-style rifle with a dark wooden stock and a lengthy, slender metal barrel. The stock is robust and curves gently, providing a solid shoulder rest, while the barrel extends straight and is affixed with several metal bands. The lock mechanism is visible and appears to be of a flintlock or percussion cap design, with a hammer and trigger assembly. The rifle features minimal ornamentation, with a utilitarian finish and some metallic highlights along the barrel and lock. The overall appearance suggests it is a historical firearm, likely from the 18th or 19th century, with a fixed wooden stock and iron sights. The surface shows signs of age and use, with a matte, slightly rough finish.
description: A long, antique rifle with a dark wooden stock and a slender metal barrel, featuring a classic lock mechanism and minimal ornamentation.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the rifle, highlighting the curved wooden stock and long metal barrel.
description_view_1: Front-on view displaying the narrow profile of the barrel and the end of the stock.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, again showing the full length, with the lock mechanism and trigger more visible.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the butt of the stock and the slim profile of the rifle.
